rulings.Case Title: ADRIAN FOX VS. NISSAN NORTH AMERICA, INC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Pltf'S Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One And First Set Of Special Interrogatories, Set One. Nos 5 And 7
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, August 28, 2013, Line 3, PLAINTIFFS ADRIAN FOX, WILLIAM MCMULLEN, JR., SCOTT WINKLER'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Pltf's Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One And First Set Of Special Interrogatories, Set One. Nos 5 And 7 Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to Requests for Production nos. 1, 2, 4, 5, 6 - 13, 15 - 18, 20, and 21 is granted in part. Within 10 days, Nissan North America, Inc. shall serve: (1) supplemental verified statements of compliance for Requests for Production nos. 1, 2, 4, 7, 8, 10, 13, 15, 16, 18, 20, and 21; (2) supplemental verified responses for Requests for Production nos. 6 and 9 affirming its representation that the Consumer Affairs, Salesforce.com, and Techline databases contain records of responsive communications with customers and dealers and it should not have to separately search emails for documents responsive; (3) further verified responses to Requests for Production nos. 11 and 12, which seek relevant information; (4) a further verified response to Requests for Production no. 17 by providing organizational charts and directories only; and (5) further verified responses to Requests for Productions nos. 5 and 7 that are limited to the identification of the two individuals who report to Robert Hankin and the 32 individuals who report to Randy Lieske. As to the balance of the motion, which pertains to the database search protocols, the Court will hold a further hearing on September 4, 2013 at 2:00 p.m. at which counsel and their database experts must be present to discuss the database search protocols at issue that the parties have been unable to resolve. The parties should be prepared to inform the Court, by using a statistically significant randomly selected sample, what percentage of the records in the sample retrieved utilizing all terms requested by Plaintiffs capture records responsive to Plaintiffs' document requests. Specifically, Nissan shall be prepared to inform the Court whether the number of nonresponsive records obtained by application of all 25 search terms proposed by Plaintiffs could be reduced by applying to the initial search result set further limiting terms such as "manifold intake valve" or some other limiting term or terms, such as utilizing search terms consisting of parts numbers. Counsel are ordered to continue meeting and conferring on the database search issue, and if the parties are able to resolve these issues without further assistance of the Court, this further hearing may be taken off calendar or continued. The court will not provide a court reporter for discovery hearings. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: SHEREEN PINTO et al VS. M. GAEHWILER CONSTRUCTION,INC., A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant M. Gaehwiler Construction,Inc.'S Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, August 28, 2013, Line 10, Defendant M. Gaehwiler Construction,Inc.'s Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement The Court grants Defendant and Cross-Complainant M. Gaehwiler Construction, Inc.'s ("MGC") motion for determination of good faith settlement. Requests for judicial notice are granted. Substantial evidence supports the conclusion that the settlement meets the Tech-Bilt factors. MGC has sufficiently explained the allocation, MGC's proportionate liability, and that there was no fraud or collusion between the parties. (See Declaration of Marshall in Support of MGC's Motion for Determination of Good Faith Settlement; Declaration of Williams in Support of MGC's Motion for Determination of Good Faith Settlement.) Opposing parties have failed to demonstrate that the settlement is out of the ballpark. (CCP Section 877.6(d).) Defendant Tile West, Inc.'s request to continue the hearing to conduct discovery is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: CLINTON T. REILLY, AS TRUSTEE OF THE CLINTON T. VS. RICK BELGARDE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Issue, Evidence, And Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 28, 2013, Line 3: Defendant RICK BELGARDE'S Motion for Issue, Evidence, and Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Bruce Highman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. The court is inclined to deny issue and evidence sanctions if prejudice to defendant can be mitigated. At the hearing, the court will want to discuss what documents that were requested through Requests 1-11 were not produced; will plaintiff voluntarily produce them now and if so, how quickly; who needs to be deposed or redeposed regarding the documents, and will plaintiff cooperate in voluntarily allowing the depositions and redepositions; what other discovery needs to be done to follow-up on the documents, and will plaintiff cooperate in allowing the discovery; what expenses has defendant incurred and will defendant incur because the documents were not produced earlier; if a trial continuance is needed, how long is needed, and will defendant do the work to obtain the continuance. The court is tentatively inclined to take the matter under submission and follow-up quickly with phone conferences on whether the things needed to be done to mitigate prejudice to defendant are being done. The court is inclined to grant monetary sanctions against plaintiff for the time expended on the motion to compel production of documents and the present motion because both motions have been necessitated by plaintiff's unjustified failure to produce documents. The court notes that: (1) the motion to compel documents and for sanctions for that motion was previously denied because plaintiff misrepresented to the court that there were no other documents; (2) the motion should have been granted had this misrepresentation not been made; and (3) after the court denied the motion, it was revealed through depositions that there were additional documents. The court is inclined to deny monetary sanctions for the motion to compel the depositions because the court previously denied sanctions for that motion and no new facts have come to light after that denial that would warrant reversal of the court's prior order. The court is also inclined to deny monetary sanctions for the motion to continue trial which was denied by the presiding judge. To aid the Court in determining the amount of monetary sanctions, defendant shall bring to the hearing two copies of all time records of its attorneys on matters for which he seeks sanctions. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: VALERYA SEROVAYSKAYA VS. LILYA VAISMAN DDS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Bifuricate Issues
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, August 28, 2013, Line 12, DEFENDANT KRISTIAN LUNDGREN-KOSZEGHY, DMD, MMSC, Notice Of Motion And Motion To Bifuricate Issues The motion is granted in part. Pursuant to CCP 597.5, the statute of limitations defense must be tried "separately and before any other issues in the case are tried." The Court denies without prejudice the request for separate juries. Plaintiff has not shown that separate juries will further judicial economy. This order is without prejudice to raising the issue before the trial judge. rulings.Case Title: FRESCO MUSIC, LLC VS. ALJOY, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Andm Motion To Compel Defts Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set No One And Motion For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 28, 2013, Line 1: Plaintiffs FRESCO MUSIC, LLC'S, JACOBO JUAREZ'S, and NHU TRUONG'S Motion to Compel Defendants Further Responses to Request for Production of Documents, Set No. One and Motion for Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Bruce Highman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. The court is inclined to rule as follows: Granted in part. Aljoy shall electronically produce all documents that it has already produced or is producing in response to Fresco's first set of document requests. Aljoy's electronic production shall comply with plaintiff's protocol except that native files need not be Bates stamped. Aljoy shall notify the court at the hearing whether it wants placed under a protective order any of the additional documents that it is being ordered to produce. All documents and responses that this order orders Aljoy to provide shall be provided by September 18. Requests 1-5, 29-31, and 38-39. Denied. Requests 10-11. Hearing required. Aljoy will let the Court know at the hearing if it has produced all nonprivileged responsive documents. Requests 12-17. Denied. Requests 18-19. Granted in part. Aljoy shall amend its responses to state that it is producing and shall produce all documents showing funds Aljoy transferred from SOM to Metropol or that Aljoy used to pay Metropol's debts. Request 24: Granted in part. Aljoy shall amend its response to state that it is producing and shall produce all documents related to any California Department of Alcoholic Beverage Control violations or investigations prior to the 2010 citation referred to in Request 24. Aljoy shall amend its response to state that it is producing these documents. Request 25 and 27: Denied without prejudice to more narrowly written requests. Requests 32, 34, and 36: Granted in part. Aljoy shall amend its responses to state that it is producing and shall produce all nonprivileged responsive documents that were written by or to Aljoy or that relate to SOM. Requests 33, 35, and 37: Granted in part. Aljoy shall amend its responses to state that it is producing and shall produce all responsive documents that were written by or to Aljoy or that relate to SOM. Request 40: Granted. Aljoy will produce the report that it agreed to produce in its response to Request 40. Aljoy shall also amend its response to state that it is producing and shall produce the ESI version of the August 23, 2010 letter in the manner that this Order requires it to produce ESI as indicated above. Sanctions are denied. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CENTER FOR ENVIRONMENTAL HEALTH VS. FOOD MARKET MANAGEMENT, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-12-526396, CGC-13-531045 WITH CASE CGC-12-526395
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, August 28, 2013, Line 19, PLAINTIFF CENTER FOR ENVIRONMENTAL HEALTH MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-12-526396, CGC-13-531045 WITH CASE CGC-12-526395 The motion is denied. The Court cannot order "pretrial consolidation." In Sanchez v. Sup. Crt., (1988) 203 Cal.App.3d 1391, 1396, the court explains that there can only be complete consolidation or consolidation for trial purposes. The parties are instructed to coordinate their discovery and motion practice to reduce party and court inefficiencies. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: LINDA DAVIDSON VS. MERCEDES-BENZ USA, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, August 28, 2013, Line 20, DEFENDANT MERCEDES-BENZ USA, LLC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT The Court sustains the demurrer to the 3rd cause of action for breach of the implied covenant without leave to amend. The cause of action appears to be improperly seeking tort damages and while plaintiff is entitled to contractual damages, the cause of action is duplicative of the breach of contract claim. See Careau & Co. v. Security Pacific Business Credit, Inc. (1990), 222 Cal. App. 3d 1371, 1395. The demurrer to the 5th cause of action for violation of the CLRA is overruled. Paragraphs 8, 37-38, and 42 of the amended complaint allege that defendant failed to deliver the promised warranty. Those allegations support the claim. Plaintiff's CLRA notice is timely per Morgan v. AT & T Wireless Services, Inc. (2009), 177 Cal. App. 4th 1235, 1261. The Court overrules the demurrer to the 6th cause of action for violation of the UCL. Paragraphs 8, 37-38, 42, and 46c sufficiently plead an unfair business practice. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: GREGORY SANDRITTER VS. CHARLES SMITH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Answers To Form Interrogatories, Set One, Request For Admissions, Set One, Request For Production Of Documents, Set One; And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 28, 2013, Line 2: Plaintiff GREGORY SANDRITTER'S Motion to Compel Answers to Form Interrogatories, Set One, Request for Admissions, Set One, Request for Production of Documents, Set One; and for Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Bruce Highman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. The court is inclined to rule as follows: Form interrogatory 15.1. Granted. Defendant only answered this interrogatory as to the first affirmative defense of comparative fault. Defendant does not need to answer this interrogatory as to the second affirmative defense that the complaint fails to state a cause of action. Defendant shall answer this interrogatory as to all other affirmative defenses with the interrogatory being answered separately for each affirmative defense. Request 11. Defendant shall bring all documents responsive to Request 11 to the hearing other than: (1) confidential communications between attorney and client or consulting expert; (2) attorney notes that have not been shown to anyone other than the client or a consulting expert; or (3) documents written by a consulting expert which have not been shown to anyone other than the attorney or client. Defendant shall identify at the hearing the names of all witnesses from whom the defense has obtained statements. The court might decide to examine the documents responsive to Request 11 in camera. Request 18. Defendant shall produce all responsive documents with transactions other than at the Chevron Gas Station at the corner of Harriet and Harrison Streets redacted and with all dollar amounts and account no. information redacted. All responses and documents required by this order shall be served by September 9. Sanctions are denied. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)
